entities.name: spirit (unnamed horseman)
entities.raw_quote: you will see toward the east someone coming on horseback, who, when he arrives at the circle, will say, ‘So-and-so’ – naming the names written above

entities.name: consecrated ring
entities.raw_quote: You will take the ring, saying to him, ‘Thanks to you and to them.’ When you have said this, he will at once withdraw… place this ring on his finger, and he will appear to be a corpse

entities.name: white cloth
entities.raw_quote: Keep this ring with you, wrapped in a sheet of white cloth.

entities.name: hoopoe feather and blood
entities.raw_quote: If you draw it on a Friday with the feather of a hoopoe and with its blood on a freshly prepared sheet of parchment

entities.name: double circular band with pentagram
entities.raw_quote: The figure shown for this experiment is a double circular band with a pentagram inscribed.

entities.name: Lamair
entities.raw_quote: Masair, Lotobor, Tatomofon and Sirama in the innermost…

entities.name: Oymelor
entities.raw_quote: Oymelor, Symofos, Manoir and Faubair in the outermost band

entities.name: Demefin
entities.raw_quote: Demefin, Rodobayl, Tamafin and Abelutabel in the next

entities.name: Feremni
entities.raw_quote: The nomenclature is again variable: Lantrayth appears also as Lautrayth and Lutrayth, Feremni as Feremin and Feremim

entities.name: Antichrist
entities.raw_quote: pretending to resurrect the dead was one specialty associated traditionally with Antichrist

entities.name: Honorius of Regensburg
entities.raw_quote: Honorius of Regensburg in his Elucidarium had his disciple ask whether Antichrist will truly raise the dead

entities.name: Hildegard of Bingen
entities.raw_quote: Hildegard of Bingen agreed that Antichrist’s miracle is an illusion

attributes.content: conjuration of spirit in equine form

entities.name: spiritus equus
entities.raw_quote: vocaui istos eosdem spiritus, et eundem spiritum ve[l] equum michi feci apportari

entities.name: Clm 849
entities.raw_quote: a warning against the sign of the cross outside the context of flight occurs also in Clm 849, no. 1

summary: This chunk details necromantic experiments from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), focusing on a spirit-conjuration rite involving a horseman who delivers a consecrated ring used to control life/death appearances. It includes descriptions of protective circles, ritual ingredients (hoopoe blood and feather), named spirits inscribed on magical rings, and theological reflections on Antichrist’s deceptive miracles. The text also references parallel traditions in Rawlinson MS D 252 and compares medieval illusionist magic to theological debates about demonic deception.
key_passages: you will see toward the east someone coming on horseback, who, when he arrives at the circle, will say, ‘So-and-so’ – naming the names written above – ‘send you this consecrated ring…'

When you wish a corpse to appear animated, place the ring as aforesaid… and within an hour it will arise in the form to which it is previously accustomed, and will speak before all with a living voice, and will be able to display this quality for six days
If you draw it on a Friday with the feather of a hoopoe and with its blood on a freshly prepared sheet of parchment…
The figure shown for this experiment is a double circular band with a pentagram inscribed. The names carved on the ring are repeated, along with astrological signs, within the two bands.
pretending to resurrect the dead was one specialty associated traditionally with Antichrist.
